description_long: This object is a double-viewed axe, featuring a robust, dark-colored handle with a pronounced curve, likely made of wood or a composite material. The axe head is broad and heavy, with a single cutting edge and a thick poll, suggesting it is designed for chopping or splitting rather than fine carving. The blade has a slightly convex edge and a deep beard, providing a large cutting surface and additional weight for powerful swings. The mounting of the blade to the handle appears traditional, with the head fitted over the top of the handle and possibly wedged in place. The surface of the handle is smooth with visible fasteners or rivets, enhancing grip and durability. No visible markings, engravings, or brand labels are present on either the handle or the blade.
description: A heavy, curved-handled axe with a broad, single-edged blade and a smooth, dark finish. The design is robust and functional, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from the side, highlighting the curved handle and the full profile of the broad blade with its convex edge.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, showing the thinness of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side view reveals the poll and the attachment of the blade to the handle, as well as the curvature of the handle.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the other edge, emphasizing the thickness of the blade and the handle's profile.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, elongated handle made of dark wood or composite, smooth finish, oval cross-section, no visible texturing or finger grooves.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, single-edged blade with a convex edge and deep beard, thick poll, no decorative features, functional geometry for chopping.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting, blade fitted over the top of the handle.
